title: Conditional formatting "Data Bar" example
author: L1Odev
body:
## 💬 Questions and Help

Please, can you provide any example how to use Data Bar option in Conditional formatting?
https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs#data-bar

I tried this
`
worksheet.addConditionalFormatting({
      ref: 'A1:E7',
      rules: [
        {
          priority: 0,
          type: 'dataBar',
          minLength: 0,
          maxLength: 100,
          showValue: true,
          gradient: false,
          border: true,
          negativeBarColorSameAsPositive: true,
          negativeBarBorderColorSameAsPositive: true,
          axisPosition: 'auto',
          direction: 'leftToRight',
          cfvo: [{type: 'percent', value: 0}],
          style: {fill: {type: 'pattern', pattern: 'solid', bgColor: {argb: 'FF00FF00'}}},
        }
      ]
    });
`

but after saving my excel file always invalid 

error message (in russian)
`<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="true"?>

-<recoveryLog xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main">

<logFileName>error021240_01.xml</logFileName>

<summary>Обнаружены ошибки в файле "D:\PivotGrid (38).xlsx"</summary>


-<repairedParts>

<repairedPart>Восстановленный компонент: часть /xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml с ошибкой XML. Разрушительный сбой Строка 2, столбец 0.</repairedPart>

</repairedParts>

</recoveryLog>`

comments.author: BjoernBoba
comments.body:
Hi,

were you able to solve this and create a working example in the meantime?
Because I am also struggling to find a working solution.

Thanks :-)

comments.author: tearfur
ctheiss
lamminhthien
comments.body:
> This databar works for me: values are clamped to -20,000...20,000 and show red for negative, green for positive, with a dark gray axis in the middle.
> 
> ```js
> ws.addConditionalFormatting({
>      ref: `D2:D${sheet.rowCount}`,
>      rules: [
>          {
>              type: "dataBar",
>              gradient: false,
>              border: false,
>              negativeBarColorSameAsPositive: false,
>              cfvo: [ { type:'num', value:-20000 }, { type:'num', value:20000 } ],
>              color: { argb:'40DD4A' },
>              negativeFillColor: { argb:'FF0000' },
>              axisColor: { argb:'4D4D4D' }
>          }
>      ]
>  });
> ```
> 
> I figured this out by examining the code here:
> 
> https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/ec92cb3b898bdf7f806ff9d7b8370c955ee8ba20/lib/xlsx/xform/sheet/cf-ext/databar-ext-xform.js#L36-L59
> 
> and comparing with an "unzipped" XLSX file.

Thanks you so much,
I can use border from
